notes: Asserts the autonomous loop's graceful-termination shape for gemini-cli: working -> ready across >=2 turns, the session reaches ready even though the unsatisfiable objective was never met, and it never sticks in working. Gemini has no /goal self-prompt primitive, so the recipe supplies the iterations as successive prompts toward an impossible objective, then a terminal 'done' turn; the state arc is identical to a real iteration-cap hit (end-of-turn -> ready, no further self-prompting). Strict invariant: the session never enters waiting (no permission gate) and the final state is ready, not a stuck working span.

text: Session appears in ready (anchor UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession row can't steal the birth)
First prompt toward the impossible objective drives ready -> working
First iteration ends with a tool-call-free attempt; turn settles working -> ready. The objective is not met.
Second prompt continues the loop ready -> working
Second iteration ends; turn settles working -> ready. The agent has still not satisfied the impossible objective.

Spec: 'state reaches ready even though the agent didn't fully succeed' — the terminal turn abandons the unsatisfiable objective and replies 'done'. State settles to ready and stays there: no further self-prompting, no working flicker that would indicate a stuck loop.
